clauses.govai_mapping: Repository scope boundaries and product layering help operators document organizational context and system boundaries; GovAI does not perform context analysis on your behalf.
clauses.summary: Understanding internal and external issues and defining the scope of the AI management system.
clauses.title: Context of the organization

clauses.govai_mapping: Human approval and override narratives model accountable decision points; leadership commitments and roles remain organizational artefacts outside the repository.
clauses.summary: Top management demonstrates leadership and commitment to the AI management system.
clauses.title: Leadership

clauses.govai_mapping: Planning guides and risk narratives cross-link evaluation planning with audit evidence concepts; deterministic validators support objective-setting evidence packs, not management system certification.
clauses.summary: Actions to address risks and opportunities, AI objectives, and planning of changes.
clauses.title: Planning

clauses.govai_mapping: Contributor and implementer documentation supports competence and awareness for teams adopting GovAI tooling; HR competence records stay with the operator.
clauses.summary: Resources, competence, awareness, communication, and documented information for the AI management system.
clauses.title: Support

clauses.govai_mapping: Operational integration and evidence-pack interchange describe how AI lifecycle events are captured; runtime enforcement modes depend on deployment configuration.
clauses.summary: Operational planning and control for AI systems, including lifecycle and impact considerations.
clauses.title: Operation

clauses.govai_mapping: Standards evaluation harness and model assurance reporting supply deterministic internal measurement inputs; internal audit programmes are operator-owned.
clauses.summary: Monitoring, measurement, analysis, evaluation, internal audit, and management review.
clauses.title: Performance evaluation

clauses.govai_mapping: Incident escalation guidance and phase reports document improvement-oriented engineering work; corrective action closure remains with the management system owner.
clauses.summary: Nonconformity, corrective action, and continual improvement of the AI management system.
clauses.title: Improvement

clauses.govai_mapping: Portable capability policy interchange supports documented AI governance policies; policy content and approval are customer-controlled.
clauses.summary: Establish, implement, and maintain AI policies appropriate to the organization.
clauses.title: AI policies

clauses.govai_mapping: Model risk documentation and assurance levels frame technical risk assessment evidence; ISO 42001 risk treatment decisions are not automated by GovAI.
clauses.summary: Assess AI risks and treat them in line with the AI management system.
clauses.title: AI risk assessment

clauses.clause_reference: Annex A — Data for AI systems
clauses.govai_mapping: Dataset governance and evidence-quality narratives help operators document data practices; GovAI does not certify dataset legal sufficiency.
clauses.summary: Manage data used in development and operation of AI systems.
clauses.title: Data for AI systems

clauses.govai_mapping: Human oversight documentation aligns runtime safety and regulatory narratives with ledger-backed approval events where operators configure them.
clauses.summary: Ensure appropriate human oversight of AI systems.
clauses.title: Human oversight

clauses.govai_mapping: Audit exports and operational intelligence docs describe monitoring hooks; production telemetry configuration is deployment-specific.
clauses.summary: Monitor and measure AI system performance and impacts.
clauses.title: Monitoring and measurement

summary: Indicative ISO/IEC 42001:2023 clause and Annex A control mappings to GovAI repository artefacts. Readiness support only — not certification or legal compliance.
